channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: An explosion has caused several injuries and damaged apartments in the Dutch capital
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — An explosion rocked a neighborhood in Dutch capital early Saturday, destroying several apartments and injuring multiple people, according to authorities. The cause of the disaster in The Hague is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean lawmakers are deciding whether to impeach the president over his martial law bid
item.description: South Korean lawmakers have begun meeting to vote on whether to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived attempt to impose martial law, as protests grew nationwide calling for his removal.
item.dc:creator: Kim Tong-Hyung And Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea's governing party head supports suspending Yoon's powers, making impeachment more likely
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s governing party chief expressed support Friday for suspending the constitutional powers of President Yoon Suk Yeol for imposing martial law this week, in a bombshell reversal that makes Yoon’s impeachment more likely.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA returning to China more than five years after Daryl Morey Hong Kong tweet
item.description: The National Basketball Association (NBA) will return to China for two preseason games in 2025, more than five years after former Houston Rockets general manager Daryl Morey spurred a break in the relationship with an image supporting Hong Kong protesters.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Iran launches rocket with heaviest-ever payload into space amid heightened concern over nuclear program
item.description: Iran launches rocket into space carrying its heaviest-ever payload, as security experts remain concerned by its "drastically" increased stockpiles of enriched uranium near nuclear weapons grade purity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin signs agreement offering Russia's security guarantees to ally Belarus
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin visited Belarus on Friday to sign a treaty offering security guarantees for Moscow’s closest ally, including the possible use of Russian nuclear weapons to help repel an aggression.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Under assault by gangs in Haiti, a Kenyan-led mission fights back
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — An armored vehicle carrying Kenyan police rumbles through Haiti's capital with a shattered window that a high-caliber bullet failed to penetrate, a permanent sign of violence the officers face almost daily in Port-au-Prince.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romanian court throws out presidential results after Russian interference allegations
item.description: Romania’s top court on Friday wiped out results from the first round of presidential elections and called for a rerun, days after declassified Romanian security documents alleged Russian interference that promoted a far-right outsider candidate.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Ailing kids wait months for Israeli permission to leave Gaza for treatment. Some die in the meantime
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The 12-year-old Palestinian boy was lying in a hospital bed in central Gaza, wracked with leukemia, malnourished and whimpering in pain despite the morphine doctors were giving him, when Rosalia Bollen, a UNICEF official, said she saw him in late October.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where Turkey stands as opposition insurgents advance in neighboring Syria
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — As forces opposed to Syrian President Bashar Assad make swift and significant advances in Syria, Turkey is watching closely for any spillover.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's top court annuls first round of presidential vote won by far-right candidate
item.description: A top Romanian court has annulled the first round of the country’s presidential election, days after declassified intelligence alleged Russia ran a coordinated online campaign to promote the far-right outsider who won the first round.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump heads to France as world leaders seek to curry favor
item.description: President-elect Trump is holding court with foreign leaders as they seek to gain influence and insight into his mindset before he takes office, with the expectation that the Trump administration will bring major shifts to the global economy and to its approach to alliances.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: In the 6-hour mayhem of South Korea's martial law, a woman's act of resistance was a defining moment
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In the groundswell of fury and defiance that erupted among South Koreans after their president declared martial law, curtailing the country's hard-won freedoms, it was perhaps the iconic moment.
item.dc:creator: YONG HO KIM and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas official says Gaza ceasefire talks have resumed after weekslong hiatus
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — A Hamas official said Thursday that international mediators have resumed negotiating with the militant group and Israel over a ceasefire in Gaza, and that he was hopeful a deal to end the 14-month war was within reach.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Assad regime under major pressure as Islamist rebels seize more territory; Israel, US watch
item.description: The conflict in Syria has escalated dramatically, and Islamist rebels have caught many by surprise with rapid territorial gains at the expense of the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean president to face impeachment vote this weekend over martial law order, lawmakers say
item.description: South Korean opposition lawmakers will hold a vote to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol after the president declared martial law on Tuesday, citing "anti-state" activities by his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The world’s tallest snowman, measuring 122 feet, built by residents in Bethel, Maine
item.description: In 2008, the residents of Bethel, Maine, spent a month creating what is still to this day the tallest snowwoman that has ever been made, according to Guinness World Records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean president's martial law declaration a 'blunder,' could embolden North Korea, expert says
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol declared martial law and now faces impeachment over the surprising move from opposition lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Voters want more US involvement on world stage despite isolationist talk, Ronald Reagan Institute survey finds
item.description: The Ronald Reagan Institute released its sixth annual National Defense Survey and found 'record high' support for U.S. involvement abroad despite a growing sense that isolationism could be on the rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes on a Gaza tent camp kill at least 21 people, hospital says
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes tore through a tent camp for displaced Palestinians in southern Gaza on Wednesday, sparking fires and killing at least 21 people, according to the head of a nearby hospital, in the latest assault on a sprawling tent city that Israel designated a huma
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: French government collapses after no-confidence vote passes against Prime Minister Michel Barnier
item.description: Prime Minister Michel Barnier is expected to resign after the National Assembly passed a vote of no-confidence on Wednesday, effectively collapsing the current government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK killer nurse Letby questioned over further baby deaths
item.description: Lucy Letby, a former British nurse who is serving a life sentence for the murders of babies in her care, has reportedly been questioned about more infant deaths.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers see 'bad guys on both sides' in Syria offensive
item.description: Senators on both sides of the aisle are holding back from cheering rebel gains against Syrian President Bashar Assad, following a lightning offensive over the weekend led by Hayat Tahrir al-Sham (HTS), a U.S.-designated terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: US 'seriously concerned' by developments in South Korea
item.description: The United States was surprised by South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol’s brief declaration of emergency martial law.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with Hungary’s effort to end Holocaust survivor lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday probed the international consequences of allowing a group of Holocaust survivors and their heirs to leverage U.S. courts to seek compensation from Hungary for confiscating their property.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean parliament overrides president's martial law order
item.description: National Assembly Speaker Woo Won Shik declaring that lawmakers “will protect democracy with the people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 11 in Lebanon after fire exchanges with Hezbollah that test ceasefire’s limits
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel unleashed its largest wave of airstrikes across Lebanon since agreeing to a ceasefire with Hezbollah last week, killing at least 11 people on Monday after the Lebanese militant group fired a volley of projectiles as a warning over what it said were Israeli truce violations.
item.dc:creator: NATALIE MELTZER and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hong Kong launches panda sculpture tour as the city hopes the bear craze boosts tourism
item.description: Thousands of giant panda sculptures will greet residents and tourists starting Saturday in Hong Kong, where enthusiasm for the bears has grown since two cubs were born in a local theme park.
item.dc:creator: Kanis Leung, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elton John says he has lost his eyesight, struggles to see new musical
item.description: Elton John says he struggled to watch his new musical because he has lost his eyesight after contracting an infection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
